headline: Here are the 30,000 songs Sony is suing Udio’s AI music generator over
summary: Sony Music is suing AI music generator Udio for copyright infringement. The lawsuit cites over 30,000 songs, including tracks by Elvis, Beyoncé, and Harry Styles. It was filed in a New York court on Monday.

headline: The FCC is planning to retroactively ban disguised DJI gadgets
summary: The FCC plans to use its power to retroactively ban gadgets for the first time. It targets "DJI front companies" distributing disguised devices. This aims to crack down on previously approved but problematic tech.

headline: The Odyssey turned me into an IMAX believer
summary: The author reviewed Christopher Nolan's film, "The Odyssey." Initial impressions were mixed regarding fantastical set pieces. Despite minor conflicts, the film ultimately solidified the author's belief in IMAX.

headline: SpaceX in your index fund, explained
summary: Index funds are typically seen as a safe investment strategy. The article questions SpaceX's fast-tracking into the Nasdaq-100. SpaceX is described as a "giant gamble" and "terribly overpriced" by the author.

headline: Leon Kennedy Voice Actor Says Metallica, Nirvana Helped Shape His Performance for the Resident Evil Character
summary: Nick Apostolides revealed bands that influenced his Leon Kennedy portrayal. He cited Metallica and Nirvana as surprising playlist picks. These choices helped shape his performance in Resident Evil Remake games.

headline: Kingdom Hearts Missing-Link Leak Has Fans Wishing Square Enix Hadn't Canceled Its Mobile Spinoff
summary: Footage of the canceled mobile game "Kingdom Hearts Missing-Link" has leaked online. The leak appears to show a nearly finished intro for the game. Fans are expressing regret over Square Enix's decision to cancel the spinoff.

headline: Nier Creator Yoko Taro Mourns Death of Physical Media as PlayStation Plans to End Production of Game Discs
summary: Nier creator Yoko Taro laments the decline of physical media. His comments follow Sony's decision to end game disc production. PlayStation will cease producing game discs starting January 2028.

headline: Pick Up a Powerful and Infinitely Reusable Cordless Electric Air Duster for $19 After Coupon
summary: Get a powerful cordless electric air duster for just $19. This device is infinitely reusable for cleaning electronics like PCs. Your dusty computer will thank you for this purchase.

headline: Burnham pledges to 'bring back hope' as he makes Healey chancellor
summary: New Prime Minister Andy Burnham pledges to "bring back hope." He has appointed Healey as his new chancellor. Burnham promises announcements this week to offer people "some breathing space."

headline: Who's in Andy Burnham's cabinet?
summary: New Prime Minister Andy Burnham has finalized his cabinet appointments. The article provides an in-depth look at who secured key roles. It details which Labour MPs have been appointed to cabinet positions.

headline: Trump slaps 50% tariffs on Canada and Carney vows to 'intensify' trade talks
summary: Trump has imposed steep 50% tariffs on Canada. This marks a major escalation in trade tensions between the two North American neighbors. Carney vows to "intensify" trade talks to address the dispute.

headline: US launches fresh strikes on Iran, as Trump warns of retaliation for deaths of soldiers
summary: The US has launched new strikes against Iran. Trump warns of retaliation following the deaths of US soldiers. The attacks aim to degrade Iran's capabilities to target ships in the Strait of Hormuz.
